definition: The dollar is 8-15% overvalued on a trade-weighted Behavioral Equilibrium Exchange Rate (BEER) basis, adjusting for relative productivity and terms of trade. PPP metrics suggest more extreme overvaluation: ~15-20% vs EUR, ~25-35% vs JPY, ~5-10% vs GBP. BEER models have documented out-of-sample predictive power at 1-5 year horizons, but the exorbitant privilege can sustain overvaluation for years.
evidence: OECD PPP estimates: 15-20% overvalued vs EUR, 25-35% vs JPY, 5-10% vs GBP
BEER model adjusting for productivity and terms of trade narrows to 8-15% trade-weighted
Cheung et al. (2005): BEER models have documented out-of-sample predictive power at 1-5 year horizons
Exorbitant privilege and safe-asset shortage may sustain overvaluation indefinitely
open_questions: Whether the exorbitant privilege has permanently shifted fair value metrics
Whether AI capex inflows represent a structural productivity shift that justifies some of the overvaluation
Time horizon over which BEER mean-reversion exerts force
